SEARCH
Package com.dua3.utility.fx.controls

Class ToolBarEx

java.lang.Object
javafx.scene.Node
javafx.scene.Parent
javafx.scene.layout.Region
javafx.scene.control.Control
javafx.scene.control.ToolBar
com.dua3.utility.fx.controls.ToolBarEx
All Implemented Interfaces:
DetachableNode<ToolBarEx,javafx.scene.Parent>, javafx.css.Styleable, javafx.event.EventTarget, javafx.scene.control.Skinnable
public class ToolBarEx extends javafx.scene.control.ToolBar implements DetachableNode<ToolBarEx,javafx.scene.Parent>
A subclass of ToolBar that implements the DetachableNode interface, allowing it to be dynamically moved or detached within an application's UI. It introduces support for managing the toolbar's location using a Property object.

ToolBarExt enables flexibility in the UI layout by allowing the toolbar to exist in different states such as hidden, embedded, part of the application, or in an independent floating window.

    Methods inherited from interface javafx.css.Styleable

    getStyleableNode

  • Property Details

    • location

      public javafx.beans.property.Property<DetachableNode.Location> locationProperty
      Provides access to the property representing the location or visibility state of the toolbar. This property allows observing and modifying the Location of the toolbar, indicating whether it is hidden, embedded, part of the application, or in a floating window.
      See Also:
      • getLocation()
      • setLocation(DetachableNode.Location)
      • locationProperty()

    • applicationParent

      public javafx.beans.property.Property<@Nullable javafx.scene.Parent> applicationParentProperty
      Returns the property representing the parent of this toolbar in the application context.
      See Also:
      • setApplicationParent(Parent)
      • applicationParentProperty()

  • Constructor Details

    • ToolBarEx

      public ToolBarEx(javafx.scene.Node... items)
      Constructs a ToolBarExt instance with the provided items. This constructor initializes the toolbar with the specified Node elements and adds them as children to the toolbar.
      Parameters:
      items - one or more Node elements to be included in the toolbar.

  • Method Details

    • addLocationListener

      public void addLocationListener(DetachableNode.LocationListener listener)
      Description copied from interface: DetachableNode
      Registers a DetachableNode.LocationListener to receive updates when the location or visibility state of a DetachableNode changes.
      Specified by:
      addLocationListener in interface DetachableNode<ToolBarEx,javafx.scene.Parent>
      Parameters:
      listener - the LocationListener instance to be notified of location changes; must not be null.

    • removeLocationListener

      public boolean removeLocationListener(DetachableNode.LocationListener listener)
      Description copied from interface: DetachableNode
      Removes a previously registered location listener from the node. The location listener will no longer receive updates when the node's location changes.
      Specified by:
      removeLocationListener in interface DetachableNode<ToolBarEx,javafx.scene.Parent>
      Parameters:
      listener - the DetachableNode.LocationListener to be removed; must not be null and should already be registered
      Returns:
      true if the listener was contained in the list of listeners

    • setLocation

      public void setLocation(DetachableNode.Location location)
      Sets the value of the location property.
      Specified by:
      setLocation in interface DetachableNode<ToolBarEx,javafx.scene.Parent>
      Property description:
      Provides access to the property representing the location or visibility state of the toolbar. This property allows observing and modifying the Location of the toolbar, indicating whether it is hidden, embedded, part of the application, or in a floating window.
      Parameters:
      location - the value for the location property
      See Also:
      • getLocation()
      • locationProperty()

      Specified by:

      Property description:
      Provides access to the property representing the location or visibility state of the toolbar. This property allows observing and modifying the Location of the toolbar, indicating whether it is hidden, embedded, part of the application, or in a floating window.
      Returns:
      the value of the location property
      See Also:

    • getNode

      public ToolBarEx getNode()
      Description copied from interface: DetachableNode
      Returns the actual UI component (e.g., JToolBar or ToolBar) so containers can add/remove it dynamically.
      Specified by:
      getNode in interface DetachableNode<ToolBarEx,javafx.scene.Parent>
      Returns:
      the actual UI component

    • locationProperty

      public javafx.beans.property.Property<DetachableNode.Location> locationProperty()
      Provides access to the property representing the location or visibility state of the toolbar. This property allows observing and modifying the Location of the toolbar, indicating whether it is hidden, embedded, part of the application, or in a floating window.
      Returns:
      a Property<Location> representing the location state of the toolbar.
      See Also:
      • getLocation()
      • setLocation(DetachableNode.Location)

    • applicationParentProperty

      public javafx.beans.property.Property<@Nullable javafx.scene.Parent> applicationParentProperty()
      Returns the property representing the parent of this toolbar in the application context.
      Returns:
      a Property<@Nullable Parent> representing the parent of the toolbar in the application.
      See Also:
      • setApplicationParent(Parent)

      Specified by:

      Property description:
      Returns the property representing the parent of this toolbar in the application context.
      Parameters:
